items.title: Vestigo confirms fire at offshore Sarawak facility, says no injuries reported
items.summary: The company said the fire occurred at approximately 2pm and that investigations into the cause are ongoing. Yesterday, the Fire and Rescue Department said a lightning strike had hit a vent stack at the offshore facility, triggering a brief gas line explosion. Vestigo said investigations into the cause of the fire were continuing.

items.title: Pahang Health Department denies viral claims, says Bentong Hospital haemodialysis unit operating as usual
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 29 — The Pahang State Health Department (JKNP) denies messages that are currently going viral on social media regarding the closure of the Bentong Hospital Haemodialysis Unit. “JKNP understands the concerns of the community, especially patients and relatives who depend on haemodialysis treatment services. JKNP also advises the public not to easily believe or spread unverified information and always refer to the official communication channels of the Bentong Hospital, JKNP or the Ministry of Health to obtain accurate information.

items.title: Police tracking down Proton X70 driver after zig-zag crash with BMW on LDP goes viral
items.summary: Subang Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Wan Azlan Wan Mamat said police received information on the viral video showing the SUV allegedly swerving in a zig-zag manner before colliding with a BMW, Harian Metro reported. He said preliminary investigations found the incident occurred at about 5pm when the BMW, travelling from Puchong towards Subang Jaya, was moving slowly in the right lane due to heavy traffic congestion. Wan Azlan said both drivers were not injured in the incident.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Tourism fallout from West Asia conflict and Langkawi’s border security readiness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — The impact of the West Asian conflict on foreign tourist arrivals to Malaysia and the readiness of assets and personnel of security agencies in Langkawi in addressing the threat of smuggling activities of migrants are among the focus of today’s Dewan Rakyat sitting. According to the Order Paper published on the official Parliament website, Dr Ahmad Fakhruddin Fakhrurazi (PN-Kuala Kedah) will ask the Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister to state the how the conflict in West Asia has affected the number of foreign tourist arrivals to Malaysia, particularly from the Middle Eastern, European and West Asian markets, and the Government’s strategies to sustain the growth momentum of the tourism sector. Meanwhile, Datuk Willie anak Mongin (GPS-Puncak Borneo) will ask the Housing and Local Government Minister to state the number of unsold affordable homes by state and price category as well as the actual rate of home ownership among young people under the age of 35 by state and Parliamentary constituency.

items.title: Teen suspect arrested in Kota Bharu raid as authorities seize RM20,000 red eagle, owls and musang pandan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 29 — A red eagle, worth RM20,000, was among the animals seized in a raid carried out by the General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade, together with the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) in Kampung Bunohan, Tumpat yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said in the raid at 3.15pm, his party also arrested an 18-year-old man and seized several protected wildlife and cages worth a total of RM48,500. “A raid at the house found a red eagle, four ruak-ruak birds (also known as the White-breasted Waterhen), a burung hantu carik kafan (short-eared owl), two musang pandan (giant flying foxes) as well as five cages.

items.title: Homes without window grilles in high-rises put families at risk, says expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The growing trend of minimalist, modern homes without window or balcony grilles in high-rise residential buildings is putting occupants and their families at unnecessary risk, an expert has warned. Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia Department of Architecture and Built Environment lecturer Associate Professor Dr Norngainy Mohd Tawil said many homeowners now prioritise aesthetics, natural lighting and ventilation, opting not to install window grilles or other protective features. Homes without grilles can still be made safe by installing features such as window restrictors, laminated safety glass, balcony railings that meet safety specifications, and modern grilles equipped with emergency-release mechanisms," she told Berita Harian.

items.title: Philippines leads the world in rush to solar as power prices soar
items.summary: MANILA: People in the Philippines are flocking to install solar power on rooftops and escape the burden of soaring electricity prices, making it the world's biggest spender on solar panels since the war in Iran started. Top power distributor Meralco has raised prices by 10 per cent since the Middle East conflict began in late February. Now, a median household spends around 12 per cent of monthly income on electricity, assuming it consumes 200 kilowatt-hours — approximately the monthly average for three people.

items.title: Diesel subsidy rush: Over 18,000 apply online for extra 100 litres under new Budi scheme
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 29 — The official Budi Madani portal has received more than 18,000 applications for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month from eligible owners of diesel-powered pickup trucks and SUVs as of 10pm yesterday. Second Finance Minister Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the early implementation of Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel), now in its second day, has proceeded smoothly without any technical issues. “From yesterday until now, more than 40,000 transactions have been recorded involving users benefiting from the Budi Diesel programme,” he told reporters after inspecting the early implementation of Budi Diesel at NKVE, Damansara Bound Petronas petrol station, with Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican.

items.title: Cops hunt Perodua Kembara driver after hit-and-run crash involving car, bike on Federal Highway (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — Police are tracking down the driver of a Perodua Kembara who allegedly drove dangerously, crashed into two vehicles and fled the scene on the Federal Highway near here on June 20. Petaling Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police detected a viral video of the incident at about 7.45pm last night before launching an investigation, Harian Metro reported. “After the crash, the driver fled the scene, leaving the vehicle behind,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Missing Taiping woman tells Kajang police she chose to vanish, begs family to let her be
items.summary: IPOH, June 29 — A woman reported missing in Taiping since June 16 has come forward and lodged a police report at the Kajang District Police Headquarters in Selangor, denying allegations that she had been abducted. Taiping District Police acting chief Supt Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said Nor Afiza Zainuddin, 41, lodged the report at about 3pm yesterday. On June 24, Mohd Ariffridzuan said Nor Afiza was last seen leaving her mother’s house in Lorong Mewah 16, Taman Mewah, Kamunting, on June 16, driving a white Perodua Axia.

items.title: #NSTviral: SMK Derma students impress netizens with professional football livestream skills [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The broadcasting skills of students from SMK Derma in Perlis have taken social media by storm, earning widespread praise from netizens. The clip, posted on TikTok by the school on June 18, shows students operating a professional livestream for a Perlis inter-school football match. @smkdermaperlis 90% of this afternoons live stream was operated by SMK Derma students!

items.title: Johor polls: PAS to decide today whether to back Bersatu, says Takiyuddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 29 — PAS will announce its position on support for Bersatu in the Johor state election after a special Central Committee meeting today, its secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said. Speaking to reporters after attending the Kota Baru PAS annual general meeting, Sinar Harian reported that Takiyuddin said the meeting, scheduled to begin at 5pm, would determine the party’s stance on several issues, including political cooperation within the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. Takiyuddin also dismissed any suggestion that the Perikatan logo could be changed for the Johor election, saying the matter had already been settled following Saturday’s nomination process.

items.title: Imported EV prices set to top RM300,000 as new rules take effect
items.summary: The policy is expected to reshape the country's EV market by removing affordable imported models from the mass market and encouraging global manufacturers to establish local assembly operations. Kenanga Research analyst Wan Mustaqim Wan Ab Aziz said the ministry imposed a minimum Cost, Insurance and Freight (CIF) value of RM200,000 for each imported EV unit entering Malaysia. The new regulations also require a minimum power output requirement of 180 kilowatts (kW) for imported EVs entering the Malaysian market.

items.title: Mother longs to look into the eyes of late son's cornea recipient
items.summary: PASIR MAS: A mother is hoping to meet the recipient of her late stepson's donated corneas, saying looking into the person's eyes would be the closest she could come to seeing him again. Nor Afini Din, 42, said she wished to meet the recipient if they were willing to come forward after reading her story. Nor Afini said the family was devastated by the loss, but found comfort in fulfilling Muhammad Jaris Amirul's wish to donate his corneas after his death.

items.title: 13 injured, no deaths in 5.5-magnitude quake in southwest China's Sichuan
items.summary: CHENGDU (China): No deaths were reported and 13 people sustained minor injuries as of 3:30Sam Monday after a 5.5-magnitude earthquake struck Gaoxian County in Yibin City, southwest China's Sichuan Province, local authorities said Monday, reported Xinhua. Relief efforts are underway in an orderly manner, according to Yibin earthquake relief command headquarters. The earthquake occurred at 12:12am Monday (Beijing Time).

items.title: Long wait over: LRT3 finally rolls into Shah Alam, bringing relief for commuters
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 29 — As station staff counted down from 10 before the shutters rolled open at exactly 6am, 27-year-old Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) student Qamarul stepped forward, tapped his Touch ‘n Go card at the gantry and became the first commuter to enter Seksyen 7 Shah Alam station as the long-awaited LRT3 Shah Alam Line began operations. He said the new rail line would make travelling around the Klang Valley much easier. Now it’ll be easier to get to Kuala Lumpur or Klang.” Qamarul, who was the first commuter to enter the Seksyen 7 Shah Alam LRT3 station, speaks to Malay Mail.

items.title: Ringgit slightly higher against greenback, supported by low oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit was slightly higher against the US dollar on Monday as the domestic unit continues to be well-supported with lower crude oil prices. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said West Texas Intermediate and Brent crude prices were hovering around US$69.87 per barrel and US$72.46 per barrel, while the United States and Iran are expected to continue their negotiation deals. "On that note, expect the US dollar/ringgit (pair) to revolve around RM4.08 to RM4.10 today," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.

items.title: Father and son rescued after four days buried under rubble of Venezuela's earthquakes
items.summary: LA GUAIRA, (Venezuela): A father and his son were pulled out alive from the rubble of a collapsed building on Sunday, four days after the devastating earthquakes that struck Venezuela. Their rescue came after 12 hours of painstaking efforts by teams that combed through the ruins using specialized search cameras, carefully working through unstable rubble to reach the trapped victims. The rescue team in that area includes members of the French Civil Security and American responders from the Fairfax County Urban Search and Rescue Team in Virginia, who the previous day rescued a mother and her 9-month-old baby.

items.title: Police hunt Kembara driver who fled after Federal Highway crash [WATCH]
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police are tracking down a 59-year-old driver who allegedly fled after causing a three-vehicle crash on the Federal Highway on June 20. Petaling Jaya police said the incident, which recently went viral on social media, occurred at about 6.10pm on the stretch of the highway heading towards Kuala Lumpur from Shah Alam. Police said efforts are ongoing to trace the driver.

items.title: Sovereign investors with US$29tril pivot to energy assets, flag dollar fears
items.summary: LONDON: Sovereign wealth funds and central banks managing US$29 trillion in assets are turning to energy assets and raising concerns about the dollar, in a portfolio reassessment driven by unprecedented geopolitical shifts, according to an Invesco survey published on Monday. Some 80 per cent of those polled said energy security and energy transition infrastructure were the most credible investments for making their portfolios more resilient and infrastructure reached nine per cent of sovereign wealth fund assets in 2026. DOLLAR, DEBT AND RISK Concerns about the dollar were "widespread and deepening" and 61 per cent of central banks polled also said that US debt levels negatively impact the dollar's long-term position as a reserve asset, up from 20 per cent in 2024.

items.title: Looting, theft in Venezuela's earthquake zone add to tragedy
items.summary: LA GUAIRA: Not even the cables inside the small store remained intact. Outbreaks of looting have hit the coastal state of La Guaira – neighbouring Caracas – much of which is now a vast mountain of rubble after Wednesday's disaster. In a video circulating on social media, a group of people pass around boxes of appliances from a collapsed store.

items.title: Iran urges US to set timeline for 'unconditional' Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Iran urged the US on Sunday to set a timetable for Israel's "unconditional" withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories under a war-ending memorandum of understanding signed between Teheran and Washington, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Baqaei said Teheran considers safeguarding Lebanon's sovereignty and territorial integrity, as well as "the dignity and security of all Lebanese people", as "essential" for the durability of any agreement related to ending the war with the US. Iran has placed "ending the war and military operations of the Zionist regime in Lebanon alongside ending the war against Iran" at the top of its demands in both the April ceasefire understanding and the June 18 memorandum, he added.

items.title: Oil climbs following renewed US, Iran strikes in Middle East
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Oil prices rose on Monday following days of tit-for-tat strikes by the US and Iran in the Middle East that underscored the fragility of their interim peace deal and again slowed energy shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude futures climbed 52 cents, or 0.67 per cent, to US$72.51 a barrel by 2313 GMT while US West Texas Intermediate crude was at US$69.94 a barrel, up 71 cents, or 1.03 per cent. Brent crude fell 10.6 per cent last week, its third weekly decline, after crude shipments through the strait rose last week to their highest level since the US-Israeli conflict with Iran began in February.

items.title: UK scraps plans to replace destroyers with focus on drone warships
items.summary: LONDON: Britain will scrap plans to replace its ageing destroyers and will instead procure at least six "Common Combat Vessels" to serve as control hubs for uncrewed systems, the Ministry of Defence said on Sunday. However, under the long-awaited Defence Investment Plan, the warships will now be replaced by hybrid Common Combat Vessels (CCV), reflecting a broader shift towards drone warfare and frontline capabilities. "These Common Combat Vessels will provide our dedicated sailors with hybrid ships that are designed and built for the increasing threats we face," Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis said in a statement.

items.title: Factories Are Returning. Workers Are Not.
items.summary: President Donald Trump has made manufacturing revival a central pillar of his political and economic agenda. American manufacturing is returning. Manufacturing jobs are not.

items.title: Carbon credits to fund Pahang peatland restoration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Pahang Peatland Restoration Project (PPRP) is banking on future carbon credit sales to finance its 60-year restoration programme covering 96,569 hectares of peat swamp forest, as it progresses towards international certification. Carbon credit project expert and oversight committee member Dr Jane Koh said PPRP is pursuing certification under the Verra Verified Carbon Standard (VCS) and Climate, Community and Biodiversity (CCB) Standards. Koh said the project is structured around a 60-year conservation model, with carbon finance providing a sustainable funding mechanism for long-term restoration work including peat rewetting, fire prevention and community development initiatives.

items.title: Yayasan Peneraju targets IC design talent push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yayasan Peneraju is ramping up efforts to develop Bumiputera talent in integrated circuit (IC) design as part of its broader strategy to support Malaysia's ambition of becoming a global semiconductor hub. Chief executive officer Ibrahim Sani said the agency aims to produce 2,000 Bumiputera IC designers, representing 20 per cent of the government's target to develop 10,000 semiconductor talents nationwide. To achieve this, Yayasan Peneraju will launch a pilot programme involving 150 Bumiputera participants, with training delivered across five to six institutions in collaboration with industry players.

items.title: Malaysian home renovation demand rising post-pandemic on lifestyle shift, says Topmix CEO
items.summary: Topmix Bhd managing director and Luma International Sdn Bhd chief executive officer Jack Teo Quek Siang said the trend has accelerated since the Covid-19 pandemic, backed supported by rising household incomes, rapid urbanisation and stronger demand for personalised homes. Teo said continued urbanisation will is expected to sustain demand for renovation services and interior building materials as more Malaysians upgrade their homes. He said added that the secondary housing market is another key demand driver, as resale homes typically require refurbishment or remodelling to meet new owners' preferences.

items.title: Rising insurance premiums squeeze e-hailing drivers
items.summary: Some observers said the industry's benchmark daily premium has risen significantly to around RM5.50 per day in 2025 from about RM3.26 per day in 2022. The Malaysian Takaful Association (MTA) said it is aware of concerns raised by e-hailing drivers over the affordability of insurance and takaful protection. MTA chief executive officer Mohd Radzuan Mohamed said the issue had been raised through industry engagements facilitated by Bank Negara Malaysia, particularly concerns over rising protection costs and the impact on drivers' earnings and participation in the sector.

items.title: UK's likely next leader Burnham to unveil plan to shift power from London
items.summary: LONDON: Andy Burnham, the Labour lawmaker expected to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister, will outline his vision for Britain on Monday, his office said, promising to change how the nation is governed with power moving from London to the regions. Burnham, who returned to Westminster earlier this month after winning a parliamentary seat, is currently the only declared candidate to take over from Starmer and could be installed in Downing Street within weeks. Starmer announced last week he would step down, just two years after winning a huge parliamentary majority for Labour, as his popularity sank.

items.title: Trump bashes Washington's likely next mayor, touts golf course project
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump lashed out Sunday at Washington's presumptive next mayor, calling her a communist who would destroy the capital city, even as he touted grandiose building projects including the refurbishment of a golf course. Trump said in a Truth Social post that he would meet with Janeese Lewis George, who prevailed in the Democratic Party mayoral primary Tuesday, all but assuring that she will win November's election in the heavily Democratic city. Trump recently has ramped up his use of the word "communist" to describe political foes, including referring to three winners of Democratic primaries last week in New York City backed by leftist Mayor Zohran Mamdani as "godless communists".

items.title: Hizbollah-allied Lebanese parliament head says US-Israel-Lebanon deal 'will not pass'
items.summary: BEIRUT: Lebanese Parliament Speaker and Hizbollah ally Nabih Berri said early Monday that the trilateral framework agreement between Lebanon, Israel and the United States would not pass as it did not guarantee Lebanon's rights. "This agreement will not pass, and it will not be implemented in its current form," Berri said in a statement shared by his party, the Amal movement, adding that it was "an agreement of 'dictates', not an agreement that preserves Lebanon's rights." The deal paves the way for Lebanese-Israeli peace and conditions Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon on Hizbollah's disarmament.

items.title: Experts optimistic LRT3 can lure Malaysians out of their cars [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: As the Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line prepares to welcome its first passengers today, public transport advocates are hopeful the rail corridor, backed by feeder buses and on-demand vans, will draw more Malaysians away from their cars. Malaysian Public Transport Users Association (4PAM) president Ajit Johl said the first-and- last-mile connectivity for the Shah Alam Line — including 40 feeder buses, Rapid On-Demand vans and Park & Ride bays — appeared adequate for now and that operators would likely scale up capacity if needed. [[nid:1475580]] "Public transport is heavily subsidised by the government.

items.title: One election, two crises, three coalitions: The Negeri Sembilan vote that could reshape Malaysian politics
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 29 — Voters in Negeri Sembilan will cast their ballots on August 1 in an election that is shaping up to be one of the most unpredictable and constitutionally significant contests in the state's history. Unlike the 2023 state polls, where Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) campaigned as allies, the two coalitions are now expected to face each other directly in a three-cornered fight alongside Perikatan Nasional (PN). Beyond the political realignment, the campaign is expected to be dominated by two unconventional pillars: a brewing constitutional crisis involving the state’s royal institution and mounting “bread-and-butter” concerns among a rapidly growing population.

items.title: Israel detonates tunnel, strikes south Lebanon as Hizbollah says has right to self-defence
items.summary: BEIRUT: The Israeli army destroyed an extensive tunnel in southern Lebanon on Sunday, with Lebanese state media reporting strikes in the area and Iran-backed Hizbollah saying it reserves the right to respond to the attacks. The incidents came despite a trilateral framework agreement signed by Lebanon and Israel under US sponsorship on Friday to pave the way for peace between the two countries and disarm Iran-backed Hizbollah. Lebanon's state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported several strikes Sunday, including on the southern city of Nabatieh, with the Lebanese health ministry saying two people were wounded by an Israeli stun grenade in the south.

items.title: Council tests Semak Berita fact-checking portal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council will trial fact-checking portal Semak Berita during the Johor election period. The council said the initiative will be evaluated after the trial. Participating media organisations will appoint primary and alternate verification officers.

items.title: 10,000 tap early access to Budi Diesel
items.summary: LANGKAWI: The early-access phase of the enhanced Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) subsidy scheme got off to a smooth start yesterday, benefiting around 10,000 people ahead of its full rollout on July 1. "Under the new system, vehicle owners can transfer their subsidised diesel quota to eligible family members through the Budi Madani Diesel portal," he added. But this new price helps diesel vehicle users a lot.

items.title: Chery boosts presence in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chinese automaker Chery says it has expanded its presence in Malaysia to 60 dealerships nationwide and launched its 10th vehicle model, three years after re-entering the market. A key milestone in Chery's expansion is the development of the RM2.2 billion Chery Smart Auto Industrial Park in Lembah Beringin, Selangor. "The automotive industry thrives on competition, and we welcome fair competition," Chery said.

items.title: US military to help Venezuela airport, seaport reopen for aid
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US military personnel were arriving in quake-hit Venezuela to help expand airport capacity and reopen a key seaport to support relief efforts, the US Southern Command said in a statement on Sunday. A US Air Force C-130 Hercules cargo aircraft was transporting personnel and equipment, and US Marine Corps MV-22 Ospreys were delivering personnel and supplies, SOUTHCOM said. Last week, the US said it was mobilizing US$150 million in aid to Venezuela.

items.title: Ampang Jaya police investigate viral Ukay Perdana burial delay, urge public to avoid speculation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — The police have received seven reports regarding the issue of body management at the Ukay Perdana Islamic Cemetery, Hulu Kelang, which went viral on Thursday, and are currently conducting an investigation into the matter. Ampang Jaya district police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said that as of 5pm today, seven police reports related to the case have been received and are still under investigation. Yesterday, Bernama reported that the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) denied that the delay in a burial process at the Ukay Perdana Islamic Cemetery, which went viral on Thursday, was caused by the Nurul Hidayah Mosque, Kampung Pandan Dalam.

items.title: Sarawak JPJ backs Julau licence aid initiative as rural motorcyclists embrace legal riding and cheaper fuel
items.summary: SARIKEI, June 28 — Participants from rural areas of the Julau parliamentary constituency expressed gratitude after obtaining their Class B2 Probationary Driving Licences (PDL) through the Driving Licence Assistance Programme (BLM), qualifying them to buy subsidised petrol for their motorcycles. Before, I could not buy fuel at subsidised rates because I lacked a licence. Now that I have a licence, I can buy subsidised petrol,” she told Bernama after the closing of the 2026 Class B2 Driving Licence Assistance Programme at the Julau Closed Stadium here today.

items.title: Anwar appeals to Johor voters, urges five‑year mandate for Pakatan candidates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has appealed to the people of Johor to give the coalition the mandate to govern the state for the next five years. “I humbly appeal to the people of Johor to give Pakatan Harapan candidates the mandate to lead the state for the next five years,” the PKR president said. PH is contesting all 56 seats in the state election.

items.title: Ops Serkap in Selangor busts forged‑documents syndicate, suspected mastermind arrested
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 28 — The Selangor Immigration Department (JIM) successfully dismantled a syndicate specialising in forging travel documents and using fake security stamps through Ops Serkap conducted yesterday. Selangor JIM Deputy Director (Control) Mohd Khusairi Kamarudin said the syndicate was found to be operating a well-planned modus operandi, targeting foreigners from abroad as their primary clientele. “During that period, the syndicate would prepare various falsified documents, including travel records and fake Temporary Employment Visit Pass (PLKS) stickers, to deceive the authorities,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Pakatan’s youngest candidate Danish Hossman takes cue from PM Anwar in Johor Lama showdown
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 28 — The resilience and staunch principles of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s struggle served as the main ‘tonic’ to inspire Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Danish Hossman Abd Rahman to enter politics at the young age of 23. Contesting the Johor Lama State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seat in the 16th Johor State Election (PRN), Danish Hossman, who is the youngest candidate, described the PH Chairman as his political ‘compass’ — one who has remained steadfast in championing the welfare of the people despite facing various storms of trials. “To be honest, I only have one idol in this political arena, Datuk Seri Anwar.

items.title: Amir Hamzah unveils RM5.5m sea ambulance, promises faster patient transfers from Langkawi
items.summary: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the initiative aimed to strengthen access to faster and more efficient emergency healthcare services, particularly for the Langkawi community and tourists. Amir Hamzah, who is also co-chairman of the Langkawi Development Authority (LADA), said the sea ambulance would be operated by Hospital Sultanah Maliha (HSM) here, with its primary role being the transfer of patients from the hospital to the mainland. He said the sea ambulance service would also ensure the health, safety and privacy of patients during transfers.

items.title: Zahid says Johor must remain BN stronghold, brushes off Puad’s recycled‑candidates criticism
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, June 28 — Johor must remain a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold and continue to serve as the coalition’s political fixed deposit in the state election, said BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said a BN victory in Johor would mark the beginning of Umno’s and BN’s tangible resurgence, while reaffirming the strength of the party, which is now 80 years old. Ahmad Zahid said the momentum to restore BN’s influence must be driven comprehensively across the state to demonstrate the enduring strength of grassroots support.

items.title: Publishers must review books with Quranic and hadis content carefully, says Jakim
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 28 — The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) has urged the National Book Council of Malaysia to continuously remind publishers to exercise utmost caution in the publication of religious books containing Quranic verses and hadis texts. Its director-general Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee said in a statement today that JAKIM, together with the state Islamic religious authorities, is ready to provide the necessary guidance to publishers of religious books to ensure compliance with Islamic requirements. Sirajuddin said the department supports the firm stance of Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Datuk Dr Mohd Na’im Mokhtar that publishers must uphold the sanctity of Islam and ensure religious books are vetted by the relevant authorities before publication.

items.title: Johor polls: Two media centres and 100 NADI set up to aid coverage, MCMC to curb provocative content, says Teo
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — The Communications Ministry, in collaboration with the Information Department (JaPen), has set up two main media centres and 100 National Information Dissemination Centres (NADI) across Johor to support media coverage of the 16th Johor state election. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the two main centres, located at Hotel Seri Malaysia in Johor Bahru and NADI Kampung Sabak Awor in Muar, operate daily from 9am to 9pm from June 26 until polling day on July 11. On telecommunications coverage during the campaign period, Teo said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) would monitor telecommunications companies to ensure internet speeds remain at optimum levels.

items.title: Pakatan fielding youngest candidate in Johor polls as part of youth‑focused push, says Fahmi
items.summary: MUAR, June 28 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is committed to advancing its youth agenda in the 16th Johor state election by translating young people’s aspirations into meaningful representation through a slate of credible young candidates. PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition had demonstrated this commitment by fielding a number of young professionals, including the youngest candidate in the election. Also present was PH candidate for the Sungai Balang seat, Ayna Soraya Badaruddin.

items.title: Police arrest six 17-year-olds over bullying of 14-year-old student in Muar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 28 — Six male students, aged 17, have been arrested to assist in investigations into a bullying incident involving a 14-year-old student at a school in Muar last May. Johor Police Chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the boys were arrested in Muar and Alor Gajah, Melaka, between 1.30 am and 2.12 am today, following a report lodged yesterday. He said the police took the bullying incident at the school seriously and would conduct a transparent and fair investigation.

items.title: Fahmi: Multi‑operator tower to improve internet coverage in Sungai Balang Darat by Q3
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the ministry had identified the need for the digital infrastructure project and had been working with CelcomDigi on its implementation since the end of last year. He said the tower would be equipped with Multi Operator Core Network (MOCN) technology, allowing all major telecommunications providers to share the network and ensuring comprehensive coverage. Insya-Allah, internet connectivity issues in several critical locations in Sungai Balang will be addressed immediately,” he said when met at the Jiwa@Komuniti Madani Sembang Santai World Cup Edition programme at Pasar Awam Parit Jawa here today.

items.title: NADI teams to reach out and empower communities through digital economy, says Fahmi
items.summary: Its Minister, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, said the move is to ensure more people receive additional information and training through programmes provided by NADI. “I will ask NADI teams to reach out and assist families to receive additional information and training, for example to become TikTok affiliates or sell products online. “I also ask that NADI be proactive in going out, not merely waiting for people to come to NADI.

items.title: Eight injured in three‑vehicle crash on North-South Expressway near Sungai Petani
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Eight people were injured in a three‑vehicle collision at Kilometre 94 of the southbound North–South Expressway this morning. According to Bernama, incident involved a lorry, a Perodua Myvi and a Toyota Noah, according to Guar Chempedak Fire and Rescue Station chief Mohd Happyzam Abd Rasid, who confirmed the details in a statement. He said firefighters received the emergency call at 8.56am and found two men in the lorry, two women in the Myvi, and a man and three women in the Toyota Noah.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast for KL, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 28 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecast to affect Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm today. In a statement today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected areas include Perlis, Penang, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda and Kulim), and Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar and Bagan Datuk). The same warning has also been issued for Sarawak, covering Bau and Kuching in Kuching Division; Serian; Samarahan; Sri Aman; Betong; Sarikei; Selangau in Sibu Division; Mukah; Song and Kapit in Kapit Division; Tatau in Bintulu Division; and Subis and Miri in Miri Division.

items.title: Pakatan files police report after party flag burned in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: PH’s candidate for the Tanjung Surat state seat, Faizul Abdul Ghani, said the incident was discovered by the head of its logistics unit at about 2am today. He said that his representative, Mohamad Saat Sukami, then lodged a report with the police and the Election Commission (EC). Yusof said police take seriously any acts of mischief, provocation or sabotage that could affect public order throughout the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing state, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on ‘recycled’ candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration. According to Mohamed Khaled, fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Proposal for 17 new Sarawak seats unlikely this year as EC procedures ongoing
items.summary: KUCHING, June 28 — The proposal to add 17 new seats to the Sarawak Legislative Assembly is unlikely to be tabled in the Dewan Rakyat this year, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said the matter must go through several processes and procedures under the Election Commission (EC), including public display exercises to allow members of the public to submit objections or feedback. “At the earliest, the EC can first display it to the public.

items.title: Onn Hafiz says open debates not Johor’s way after offer from Maszlee Malik
items.summary: SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 28 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reportedly said open debates between candidates may not be suitable for the Johor state election, adding that they could disrupt the harmony of the campaign period. According to Buletin TV3 , he said Johor’s political culture has long placed importance on decorum, restraint and orderly debate, rather than open exchanges that could turn confrontational. This comes after Pakatan Harapan’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Datuk Maszlee Malik, had expressed his willingness to debate any party, particularly Onn Hafiz, during the Johor campaign period.

items.title: Malaysia shortlists four countries for new LCS anti-ship missile system, says defence minister
items.summary: KLUANG, June 28 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has shortlisted four countries as possible suppliers of an anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project after Norway cancelled the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the selection process will focus on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational needs of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) beyond basic specifications. Mohamed Khaled said the government is reviewing the delivery timeline to avoid delays that could affect the country’s maritime defence capabilities.
